meta.notes: All confirmed entries verified against actual episode scripts. Drinks appearing in multiple episodes are deduped with episodes array.

confirmed.notes: The full recipe: tequila, coconut rum, Crème de Menthe, a smidgin of Campari, Angostura bitters, topped with grapefruit juice. Cost £7.
Ordered at the Nag's Head.
'Oi John. Giss a Remi Martin with cream soda and lots of ice, and 'arf of lager.'
Del brags: 'I said get down that cellar and sort me out a bottle of Vin Ordinaire!' - thinking it means something posh.

episodes.title: A Losing Streak
episodes.note: 'a large chivas Regal with coke!' Rodney orders Southern Comfort but Del overrides: 'That's 'alf a lager for Rodney.'

confirmed.notes: Del orders defiantly: 'a large chivas Regal with coke!' Rodney orders Southern Comfort but Del overrides: 'That's 'alf a lager for Rodney.'
'Giss a Tia Maria and a pineapple juice and, er, 'alf a lager for lover boy.'
Grandad recalls his mum: 'with her rum and pep in one hand, 20 Senior Service in the other.'

confirmed.drink: Drambuie with Lime, Soda, Ice, Lemon and Cherry
confirmed.notes: Elaborate garnish-heavy order. Barmaid gives him two halves of lager instead.
Del tries to impress in Spain: 'Garçon, dos Dubonnet pore favore. Danke schon.'
Wallace serves Del brandy but they've run out of cream soda.

episodes.title: Homesick
episodes.note: 'Tea, coffee, Pina Colada?'

episodes.title: A Slow Bus to Chingford
episodes.note: Grandad tried to get one but they'd sold out - got him a Mackeson instead

episodes.title: Danger UXD
episodes.note: 'pina colada for me, please, Michael'

episodes.title: Sickness and Wealth
episodes.note: 'A pina colada for me Nerys'

episodes.title: The Jolly Boys' Outing
episodes.note: 'a couple of pina coladas'

episodes.title: Miami Twice
episodes.note: fourteen pina coladas mentioned

confirmed.notes: Del's most frequently ordered drink across the entire series.

episodes.title: Yesterday Never Comes
episodes.note: Del offers Miranda

episodes.title: The Frog's Legacy
episodes.note: Aunt Reen orders

confirmed.notes: Offered by Del as a mixer option and ordered by Aunt Reen.
Del's homemade cocktail - actually made with gin because he ran out of tequila.
Del's usual: 'Yeah, Campari and diet coke please!'

episodes.title: Happy Returns
episodes.note: 'a Singapore Sling and half of Strongbow'

episodes.title: Dates
episodes.note: 'a Singapore sling for me and a small rum for Elton John'

episodes.title: If They Could See Us Now
episodes.note: Part of Del's big celebration round

confirmed.notes: One of Del's recurring sophisticated orders.
'Yeah, Grand Marnier and grapefruit.'
'I'll have a Malibu and tonic with some lime and 'alf of lager, please darling.'
Barmaid lists Del's recent drinks: 'Tuesday it was a Bacardi and Russian.'
'last week it was Grand Marnier and Orange.'
'the week before that was Dubonnet and Coke.'

episodes.title: To Hull and Back
episodes.note: 'He doesn't know what it is, but he likes it.'

episodes.title: Sleepless in Peckham
episodes.note: Del pours himself one at the cocktail bar. Still his go-to.

confirmed.notes: One of the signature Del drink gags. Appears in both the 1985 special and the 2003 finale.
Del warns Albert: 'You'll feel even worse if I catch you at my Courvoisier again.'

episodes.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
episodes.note: 'giss a Manhattan Mike, small rum for him. Spit in the rum.'

episodes.title: Heroes and Villains
episodes.note: 'for Rodney and a Manhattan for moi.'

confirmed.notes: Ordered in two separate episodes.

episodes.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

confirmed.notes: 'giss a banana daiquiri for us moi and Australian lager for Jumbo.' Mike: 'I only sell British lager, Del.'
June's drink of choice - she totters in with a glass of it.

episodes.title: A Royal Flush
episodes.note: Albert accidentally orders 'a bum and rack!'

episodes.title: If They Could See Us Now
episodes.note: Del orders 'A rum and Black for Bobby Crush' (Albert)

confirmed.notes: Albert's drink - famously mispronounced.
'a Malibu and cherryade for moi, and one for yourself.'
Del orders 'a bottle of Beaujolais Nouveau... a '79' - not realising Nouveau means this year's vintage.
Del's yuppie phase: 'I never liked Spitzers before, but now I'm right into 'em.' Scene of the famous bar flap fall.

confirmed.drink: Pina Colada with Ice and Alka-Seltzer
confirmed.notes: After a heavy night: 'Juan. Can I have a pina colada with ice and Alka Seltzer?'
'Can I have a large Bacardi and coke, please?'
Danny spots Del's abandoned drink: 'A Castella, a Malibu reef. Are you sure he's not around?'
Pam offers Armagnac. Del: 'Yeah, that'll do if you're out of brandy.' - not realising Armagnac IS brandy.
'Give us a peach daiquiri and a chipolata sandwich.'

episodes.title: He Ain't Heavy, He's My Uncle

confirmed.notes: 'How about a large snowball? Mike, a large snowball for Dora, please.'

episodes.title: Stage Fright
episodes.note: 'Blown it all on Cuba libres and lairy suits.'

episodes.title: If They Could See Us Now
episodes.note: Del says 'Cubre Libre' - his mangled pronunciation

confirmed.notes: Del pronounces it 'Cubre Libre'.
Del's dodgy wine sold to the Nag's Head. Mike serves the remainder of the 'Romanian Rheisling.'

candidates.notes: Strongly associated with Del's yuppie phase but NOT in any script. In Yuppy Love he actually orders Beaujolais Nouveau and Spritzers.
Often mentioned in fan lists. Google AI claims S02E05 but not found in scripts.
Fan-myth. No mention of Baileys in any script.
Speculative. Tizer appears in scripts only as a comparison, never as a drink order.
